qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: RSA signature verification bypass via improper PKCS#1 v1.5 padding validation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable function and root cause. Finding identifies RSA signature padding validation flaw in rsasign-1.2.js:251 with regex /^1f+00/, while advisory describes infinite loop in bnModInverse function in ext/jsbn2.js when processing zero/negative inputs. Different files, different functions, different vulnerability mechanisms.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe deserialization in import endpoint permits attacker-controlled code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Completely different vulnerability type and root cause. Finding identifies unsafe deserialization in import workflow, while advisory describes infinite loop in bnModInverse function. Different vulnerability class (deserialization vs. numeric computation edge case), different affected components, different security impact (RCE vs. DoS).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory (GHSA-8g7p-jf3g-gxcp / CVE-2026-4598) describes a denial-of-service vulnerability via infinite loop in the bnModInverse function (ext/jsbn2.js) when BigInteger.modInverse receives zero or negative inputs. Neither of the two qualifying findings identifies this root cause: (1) rsa_signature_forgery describes RSA signature padding validation flaw in rsasign-1.2.js:251, (2) example describes unsafe deserialization in import workflow. Both findings are unrelated to the advisory's numeric computation edge case in bnModInverse.
evidence: Advisory CVE-2026-4598: Infinite loop in bnModInverse function in ext/jsbn2.js
Advisory root cause: modInverse(0, m) or modInverse(-1, m) causes infinite loop
Advisory impact: Denial of Service (process hang)
Finding rsa_signature_forgery: RSA padding validation flaw in rsasign-1.2.js:251
Finding rsa_signature_forgery root cause: Incorrect regex /^1f+00/ for PKCS#1 v1.5 padding
Finding example: Unsafe deserialization in import endpoint
Finding example root cause: Unsafe deserialization in import workflow
No overlap in vulnerable functions, files, or root-cause mechanisms
